perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, comp any confidential and/or proprietary information, hereinafter called Proprietary Information, shall be construed to mean any information, whether disclosed in writing, orally or otherwise, that the disclosing party considers to be material to its business operations (and for CAE, as applicable , the business operations of its direct and indirect subsidiaries and affiliated companies /entities (the “CAE Group”), including, without limitation, any commercial, financial, technical, and /or marketing information, b usiness and/or strategic plans, wage and salary information, trade secrets, data, concepts, computer programs, software, designs, product specifications, drawings, processes, know -how, inventions and ideas, and any information relating to its customers, su ppliers, employees or contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, company confidential and/or proprietary data, hereinafter called Confidential Material, shall be construed to mean only written documents clearly marked as confidential by the disclosing party. This includes specifically identified trade secrets and excludes any orally disclosed information, business strategies, or customer lists.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Proprietary Information' is narrowed to 'Confidential Material' and restricted to only written documents clearly marked as confidential, and the rest are exclude, creating a contradiction with the rest of the text. This change introduces a contradiction because the original definition was broad and included orally disclosed information, business strategies, customer lists, etc. Now, the agreement uses 'Proprietary Information' and 'Confidential Material' interchangeably, but their definitions are vastly different. The text is not clear on how to handle the oral information, business strategies, customer list, etc, which were consider Proprietary Information at the beginning, but now they are not considered Confidential Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The party receiving any Proprietary Information: a) shall not use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose other than the Purpose, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; b) shall protect and keep in confidence said Pro prietary Information by using the same degree of care and safeguard as it uses to protect its own Proprietary Information of like importance, but in any event no less than a reasonable degree of care; c) shall not disclose or permit that said Proprietary Info rmation be disclosed, in any manner whatsoever, other than to its directors, officers, employees, investment advisers, legal counsel or other agents (and for CAE, as applicable , a member of the CAE Group) who have a need to know, and this, only if it is st rictly necessary to disclose said Proprietary Information to these persons for the Purpose. The receiving party undertakes to advise such persons of the confidential nature of this information and shall ensure that such persons are bound by confidentialit y undertakings; d) shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any product (hardware or software) received from the other party; e) shall not remove, overprint or deface notices of copyright or ownership, trademark logo or legend, if any, from any infor mation or material obtained from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Proprietary Information' to 'Confidential Material', restricting it to only written documents clearly marked as confidential, and excluding oral information, business strategies, and customer lists. The contradicted text states that the receiving party must protect 'Proprietary Information', which, according to the original text, includes a broad range of information beyond just written documents. The changed definition directly contradicts the contradicted text by changing the scope of what needs to be protected. The change creates a strong in-text contradiction because the parties now have different understandings of what constitutes 'Proprietary Information', leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about the obligations to protect information.

perturbation.original_text: The party recei ving any Proprietary Information: a) shall not use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose other than the Purpose, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; b) shall protect and keep in confidence said Pro prietary Information by using the same degree of care and safeguard as it uses to protect its own Proprietary Information of like importance, but in any event no less than a reasonable degree of care
perturbation.changed_text: The party receiving any Proprietary Information: a) may use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose related to research, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; b) shall protect and keep in confidence said Proprietary Information by using a minimum degree of care
perturbation.explanation: The clause now permits the use of 'Proprietary Information' for 'research' purposes without prior consent, which directly contradicts the original restriction of use only for 'the Purpose' defined earlier. Additionally, the degree of care required to protect said information is now only minimum, lowering the bar for protecting said information and creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The party receiving any Proprietary Information: a) may use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose related to research, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; b) shall protect and keep in confidence said Proprietary Information by using a minimum degree of care; c) shall not disclose or permit that said Proprietary Info rmation be disclosed, in any manner whatsoever, other than to its directors, officers, employees, investment advisers, legal counsel or other agents (and for CAE, as applicable , a member of the CAE Group) who have a need to know, and this, only if it is st rictly necessary to disclose said Proprietary Information to these persons for the Purpose. The receiving party undertakes to advise such persons of the confidential nature of this information and shall ensure that such persons are bound by confidentialit y undertakings; d) shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any product (hardware or software) received from the other party; e) shall not remove, overprint or deface notices of copyright or ownership, trademark logo or legend, if any, from any infor mation or material obtained from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction by allowing the use of 'Proprietary Information' for 'research' without prior consent, which conflicts with the original restriction. Also, the 'minimum degree of care' directly contradicts any expectation of a higher standard of protection. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition as it fundamentally alters the terms and obligations related to the use and protection of Proprietary Information. The 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text' demonstrating a clause that is contradicting itself.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party undertakes to advise such persons of the confidential nature of this information and shall ensure that such persons are bound by confidentialit y undertakings
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party is encouraged to advise such persons of the confidential nature of this information but is not required to ensure that such persons are bound by confidentiality undertakings
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the receiving party 'shall ensure' that anyone receiving the confidential information must be bound by confidentiality agreements. The change weakens this obligation to an encouragement ('is encouraged') and removes the requirement to 'ensure' confidentiality undertakings. This contradicts the intention of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies the disclosing party may have, each party acknowledges and agrees that damages would not be an adequate remedy for a breach of any of the p rovisions of this Agreement; therefore the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive (final, interlocutory and provisional or preliminary), specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this Agreement before any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the receiving party to ensure confidentiality undertakings are in place. The changed text weakens this to an encouragement, removing the 'shall ensure' requirement. The contradicted text states that 'damages would not be an adequate remedy for a breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement; therefore the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief for any threatened or actual breach'. By changing the requirement to ensure confidentiality undertakings, the change undermines the ability of the disclosing party to seek injunctive relief, which is a direct and significant conflict, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring, expressly or impliedly, any rights in or title to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder. It is agreed that NDA_English Page 2 of 4 no license and more particularly no licence under any pa tents or copyrights of any party is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of Proprietary Information hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring, expressly or impliedly, any rights in or title to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder, except for a limited license to use the Proprietary Information for internal research purposes as described in Section 2. It is agreed that no license and more particularly no licence under any patents or copyrights of any party is granted by this Agreement beyond the use for internal research purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no rights or licenses are granted. The modified text introduces an exception by granting a 'limited license' for internal research, which contradicts the initial statement. This creates ambiguity about the scope of permitted use and the rights associated with the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The party receiving any Proprietary Information: a) may use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose related to research, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; b) shall protect and keep in confidence said Proprietary Information by using a minimum degree of care; c) shall not disclose or permit that said Proprietary Info rmation be disclosed, in any manner whatsoever, other than to its directors, officers, employees, investment advisers, legal counsel or other agents (and for CAE, as applicable , a member of the CAE Group) who have a need to know, and this, only if it is st rictly necessary to disclose said Proprietary Information to these persons for the Purpose. The receiving party undertakes to advise such persons of the confidential nature of this information and shall ensure that such persons are bound by confidentialit y undertakings; d) shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any product (hardware or software) received from the other party; e) shall not remove, overprint or deface notices of copyright or ownership, trademark logo or legend, if any, from any infor mation or material obtained from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits granting any rights or licenses related to the Proprietary Information. The changed text introduces a 'limited license' for internal research, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text, states that the receiving party 'may use or duplicate said Proprietary Information, in whole or in part, for any purpose related to research, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party'. The 'limited license' directly restricts the use of the Proprietary Information stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The parties shall not be liable for disclosure or use of Proprietary Information which: a) was at the time of receipt otherwise known to t he party receiving it without any breach of an obligation of confidentiality; b) was published or is otherwise within the public knowledge or is generally known to the public at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party, or becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the recipient; c) is developed independently by the recipient or parent, subsidiary or associated companies of the recipient without recourse to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; d) becomes legally known or available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party, and without breach of this Agreement by the recipient; e) becomes available to the receiving party by inspection or analysis of products available in the market other than the products of either party; f) is so disclosed or used with the written approval of the other party; g) if, and then to the extent that disclosure is required by law to be given to a governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction, provided that the recipie nt provides to the disclosing party prompt notice of any such requirement to disclose the Proprietary Information, to permit the disclosing party to seek an appropriate remedy to prevent the disclosure or alternatively to agree to the terms of such disclos ure.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties shall not be liable for disclosure or use of Proprietary Information which: a) was at the time of receipt otherwise known to the party receiving it without any breach of an obligation of confidentiality; b) was published or is otherwise within the public knowledge or is generally known to the public at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party, or becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the recipient; c) is developed independently by the recipient or parent, subsidiary or associated companies of the recipient without recourse to the Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder; d) becomes legally known or available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party, and without breach of this Agreement by the recipient; e) becomes available to the receiving party by inspection or analysis of products available in the market other than the products of either party; f) is so disclosed or used with the verbal approval of the other party; g) if, and then to the extent that disclosure is required by law to be given to a governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction, provided that the recipie nt provides to the disclosing party prompt notice of any such requirement to disclose the Proprietary Information, to permit the disclosing party to seek an appropriate remedy to prevent the disclosure or alternatively to agree to the terms of such disclos ure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the parties will not be liable for disclosure or use of Proprietary Information if it is disclosed or used with the 'written approval' of the other party. Now, the modified text states that the parties will not be liable for disclosure or use of Proprietary Information if it is disclosed or used with the 'verbal approval' of the other party. This can create a lot of confusion, given the difficulty of proving a verbal conversation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement supersedes any prior agreements and undertakings between the parties with respect to Propri etary Information supplied to each other , and is the complete agreement of the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'written approval' for exemption from liability regarding proprietary information disclosure. The changed text replaces 'written approval' with 'verbal approval'. The contradicted text specifies that the agreement 'is the complete agreement of the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement.' Allowing 'verbal approval' directly contradicts the completeness clause, as verbal agreements are outside the written agreement, creating an in-text contradiction that undermines the enforceability and completeness of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall, unless earlier terminated as indicated hereafter , be for a term of one (1) year from the date first above mentioned, and be renewed automatically for one year periods, unless a party sends in writing to the other a notice of non -renewal , thirty days prior to the expiration of a term. The disclosing party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement should the recipient fail to comply with any of the provisions of the Agreemen t.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall, unless earlier terminated as indicated hereafter, be for an indefinite term, with automatic renewal every five (5) years, unless a party sends a verbal notice of non-renewal at least one (1) day prior to each renewal date. The disclosing party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only with the written consent of the recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The contract term is changed from a one-year term with automatic annual renewal to an indefinite term with 5-year renewals. The notification requirement is changed from 'in writing' to 'verbal notice', and the number of days is changed from 'thirty' to 'one'. Furthermore, the disclosing party can terminate the agreement only with the written consent of the recipient, when before, the disclosing party could terminate the agreement if the recipient failed to comply with any of the provisions of the Agreement, which is now no longer valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall, unless earlier terminated as indicated hereafter, be for an indefinite term, with automatic renewal every five (5) years, unless a party sends a verbal notice of non-renewal at least one (1) day prior to each renewal date. The disclosing party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only with the written consent of the recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the contract's term, renewal conditions, notification requirements, and termination rights. It shifts from a one-year term with written notice to an indefinite term with verbal notice and drastically changes the disclosing party's ability to terminate the agreement, requiring written consent from the recipient instead of termination for non-compliance. Since the changed text and the contradicted text are identical, these modifications directly contradict the original agreement's terms, creating a clear in-text contradiction according to the perturbation explanation. This makes enforcement unclear, as it's uncertain whether the original or modified terms apply, leading to potential legal issues.
